mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates persistent inflation, prompting market expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, which compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in UNRATE above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS confirms broad-based economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of quarters, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production during a high-rate environment signals stagflationary pressure, forcing equity repricing and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during high-rate periods yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or regime-split IC shows no directional advantage.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in PAYEMS reflect robust employment growth that supports household income and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below 1.2 million units signals collapsing housing demand and reduced construction activity, triggering immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in >65% of months where HOUST falls below 1200, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS combined with falling CPIAUCSL indicates successful disinflation without recession, lowering long-term discount rates and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly increases corporate output and profit margins, creating immediate positive momentum in equity markets over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.
